title: Five Islands Before Dark
synopsis: When thick fog covers the lake, Zara and her new friends must visit five magical islands before sunset to help the ferry captain find his way home.

pages.text: The ferry horn sounds three times in the fog. Zara stands at the rail. She cannot see the water below. She cannot see the sky above. Captain Moss comes over. His beard drips with tiny water drops. 'We need to visit five islands before sunset,' he says. 'The compass only works when friends help.' He hands Zara a brass compass. It glows soft blue in her hands.

illustration.description: Zara, a child with natural coily hair in two puffs, deep brown skin, and sparkling dark brown eyes, stands on a ferry deck surrounded by thick fog. Captain Moss, a bearded man in a captain's hat, hands her a glowing brass compass. The ferry's rail and deck are visible but everything else fades into white mist. Golden age illustration, Arthur Rackham inspired, detailed pen work, watercolor washes, ornate fairy tale, timeless classic, decorative borders, enchanted scenes.
illustration.alt: Zara receiving glowing compass from Captain Moss in thick fog

pages.text: The compass needle spins. Then it points left. The ferry turns. Through the fog comes a sound. Rocks are singing. Their voices rise and fall like waves. 'They're sad,' says a small voice. Zara looks down. A cricket sits on the rail. She wears a tiny red hat. 'I'm Pip,' says the cricket. 'The rocks miss the sun.' Zara nods. She understands. 'Maybe we can help,' she says. Pip jumps onto Zara's shoulder.

illustration.description: Zara, a child with natural coily hair in two puffs, deep brown skin, and sparkling dark brown eyes, leans over the ferry rail listening to singing rocks emerging from the fog. A small cricket wearing a red hat sits on her shoulder. The rocks are dark and weathered, their mouths open in song. Golden age illustration, Arthur Rackham inspired, detailed pen work, watercolor washes, ornate fairy tale, timeless classic, decorative borders, enchanted scenes.
illustration.alt: Zara listening to singing rocks with cricket Pip on her shoulder

pages.text: The compass glows brighter. It points ahead. A tree grows from a tiny island. Its flowers are every color. But they're closing up tight. 'The flowers are scared of the fog,' Pip says. A deep voice rumbles. 'Not scared. Sleepy.' A large turtle pokes his head from the water. 'I'm Sage,' he says. 'The flowers need light to stay awake.' The sun is getting lower. Zara can feel it even in the fog.

illustration.description: Zara, a child with natural coily hair in two puffs, deep brown skin, and sparkling dark brown eyes, stands at the ferry rail with Pip the cricket. Before them is a small island with one magnificent tree covered in closing multicolored flowers. Sage, a large wise turtle, surfaces in the foggy water near the island. Golden age illustration, Arthur Rackham inspired, detailed pen work, watercolor washes, ornate fairy tale, timeless classic, decorative borders, enchanted scenes.
illustration.alt: Zara, Pip, and Sage the turtle looking at flower tree island

pages.text: The third island is very strange. It's made of mirrors. They reflect the fog back and forth. A swan glides up to the ferry. Her feathers shimmer silver and white. 'I'm Luna,' she says. 'The mirrors used to show the whole lake. Now they only show fog.' Zara holds up the compass. In the mirrors, she sees it multiplied. Many compasses. All glowing. All pointing the same way. 'Two more islands,' Zara says. The sun feels warmer on her back.

illustration.description: Zara, a child with natural coily hair in two puffs, deep brown skin, and sparkling dark brown eyes, holds the glowing compass while standing near Luna, a silver-white swan. Behind them is an island made entirely of mirrors reflecting fog and multiple images of the compass. Pip sits on Zara's shoulder. Golden age illustration, Arthur Rackham inspired, detailed pen work, watercolor washes, ornate fairy tale, timeless classic, decorative borders, enchanted scenes.
illustration.alt: Zara with compass reflected in mirror island while Luna the swan watches

pages.text: The fourth island has a tall tower. A bell sits at the top. But the bell is silent. A small dragon lands on the ferry. His scales are gold and green. 'I'm Echo,' he says. 'The bell used to ring every hour. Now the fog muffles everything.' Zara looks at her new friends. Pip, Sage, Luna, and Echo. The compass is glowing very bright now. 'The fifth island,' she says. 'We need to find it together.' The sun is almost setting. She can see orange light through the fog.

illustration.description: Zara, a child with natural coily hair in two puffs, deep brown skin, and sparkling dark brown eyes, stands surrounded by all her friends on the ferry deck: Pip the cricket with red hat on her shoulder, Echo the small gold-green dragon perched nearby, while Sage the turtle and Luna the swan are visible in the water beside the boat. A tall bell tower emerges from fog on a nearby island. The compass glows very brightly in Zara's hands. Golden age illustration, Arthur Rackham inspired, detailed pen work, watercolor washes, ornate fairy tale, timeless classic, decorative borders, enchanted scenes.
illustration.alt: Zara surrounded by all her magical friends near the bell tower island

pages.text: The compass needle spins fast. Then it stops. It points straight up. Above them, through the fog, a lighthouse appears. But its light is out. 'That's it!' says Captain Moss. 'The lighthouse guides me home.' All the friends work together. Pip clicks her song. Sage blows bubbles that catch light. Luna spreads her wings wide. Echo breathes gentle fire. Zara holds the compass high. The lighthouse begins to glow. The fog starts to clear. The sun sets just as they reach the dock. Captain Moss smiles. 'Friends always help friends find their way.'

coverPrompt.description: A small ferry boat floating in thick, swirling fog on a mysterious lake. Zara, a child with natural coily hair in two puffs, deep brown skin, and sparkling dark brown eyes, stands at the bow holding a glowing compass. Golden age illustration, Arthur Rackham inspired, detailed pen work, watercolor washes, ornate fairy tale, timeless classic, decorative borders, enchanted scenes.
coverPrompt.alt: Zara on a ferry boat in foggy lake holding magical compass

questions.question: What did Captain Moss give to Zara?
questions.explanation: Captain Moss gave Zara a brass compass that glowed blue in her hands.

questions.question: What was special about the rocks on the first island?
questions.explanation: The rocks on Singing Rock Island were singing with voices that rose and fell like waves.

questions.question: Which friend was a cricket?
questions.explanation: Pip was the cricket who wore a tiny red hat and sat on Zara's shoulder.

questions.question: What happened when all the friends worked together?
questions.explanation: When all the friends used their special abilities together, the lighthouse began to glow and the fog cleared.

questions.question: Why did they need to finish before sunset?
choices: To help Captain Moss get home
questions.explanation: Captain Moss needed the lighthouse to guide his ferry home, and they had to light it before dark.
